claim: Populations of wild deer across North America have tested positive for SARS-CoV-2 antibodies.

result.claim: Populations of wild deer across North America have tested positive for SARS-CoV-2 antibodies.
result.reasoning: Paper ID 2, "Susceptibility of White-Tailed Deer (Odocoileus virginianus) to SARS-CoV-2", directly states that white-tailed deer, a species prevalent in North America, are susceptible to SARS-CoV-2 infection and develop neutralizing antibodies. The abstract explicitly mentions, "All inoculated and indirect contact animals seroconverted and developed neutralizing antibodies as early as day 7 p.i." and identifies white-tailed deer as a "wild animal species susceptible to the virus." This provides strong evidence that populations of wild deer have tested positive for SARS-CoV-2 antibodies.

evidence.text: Fetal double-positive T cells (CD4+CD8+) were recovered in the maternal thymus during pregnancy.
evidence.source: Pregnancy Allows the Transfer and Differentiation of Fetal Lymphoid Progenitors into Functional T and B Cells in Mothers
